channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration flies 10-year-old back from Cuba amid custody fight
item.description: President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.

(Image credit: Hannah Schoenbaum)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
item.description: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.
item.content:encoded:
The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.

(Image credit: John Raby)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
item.description: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.
item.content:encoded:
Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
item.description: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.content:encoded: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire
item.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.
item.content:encoded:
Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare/AFP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: How the airline industry could be impacted if Spirit Airlines is liquidated
item.description: Spirit has not confirmed a liquidation, though it has filed for bankruptcy protection twice. Experts predict rising fuel costs could push the company to close its doors for good.
item.content:encoded:
Spirit has not confirmed a liquidation, though it has filed for bankruptcy protection twice. Experts predict rising fuel costs could push the company to close its doors for good.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Trump administration delays rule aimed at improving disability access in schools
item.description: Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.
item.content:encoded:
Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: With Virginia vote, Democrats gain edge over Trump's national GOP redistricting push
item.description: After Virginia voters weighed in on Tuesday, the redistricting set off by President Trump to help the GOP in the midterms has been countered and possibly surpassed by Democrats.
item.content:encoded:
After Virginia voters weighed in on Tuesday, the redistricting set off by President Trump to help the GOP in the midterms has been countered and possibly surpassed by Democrats.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Larry Kaplow

item.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
item.description: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: From the Himalayas to Newt Gingrich, the 'tree-huggers' prevail
item.description: On Earth Day, we take a look back at the rocky history of "tree-huggers." The term originated in the 1970s in the Himalayas and was later co-opted by American politics in the 1990s. Now, environmentalists are reclaiming the word.
item.content:encoded:
On Earth Day, we take a look back at the rocky history of "tree-huggers." The term originated in the 1970s in the Himalayas and was later co-opted by American politics in the 1990s. Now, environmentalists are reclaiming the word.

(Image credit: Niranjan Shrestha)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
